title: 10 best herbal teas for phlegm
intro_desc: Herbal teas for phlegm are infusions made from various plants and herbs that help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory ailments, such as congestion, coughing, and excessive mucus production. These teas contain natural anti-inflammatory, expectorant, and soothing properties that work to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thin out mucus, and soothe irritation. Examples of herbal teas that help with phlegm include thyme, eucalyptus, licorice root, ginger, and slippery elm. By drinking these teas, individuals can experience relief from respiratory discomfort, allowing them to breathe easier and sleep better. For instance, a study published in the Journal of Ethnopharmacology found that thyme tea was effective in reducing the severity and duration of cough and mucus production in patients with acute bronchitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with phlegm because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The herb's mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates, create a protective barrier that coats the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with excess phlegm production. As the tea is sipped, its soothing properties help to loosen and clear out phlegm, making it easier to expel, providing relief from congestion and coughing.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains thick, protective mucilages that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and chest, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by excess phlegm.
Saponins: The saponin content in marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion caused by phlegm buildup in the airways.

Glycyrrhizin: Marshmallow root's glycyrrhizin compound has been shown to have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the lungs and respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a popular choice for making medicinal teas as they contain high amounts of mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm coughs associated with phlegm.
Bark: The bark is also used to make teas that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marshmallow roots according to botanical guidelines and clean thoroughly.
Chop the root material into small pieces weighing approximately one ounce per eight ounces water.
Combine chopped roots with boiling water in a pot or tea infuser for seven to ten minutes steeping time.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids immediately.
Serve warm or at room temperature after allowing the tea to cool slightly if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein teas helps with phlegm because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have natural expectorant properties. The tea's mucilage, a thick, soothing substance, coats and softens excess mucus in the throat and lungs, making it easier to expel. Additionally, mullein's volatile oils help to break down and dissolve phlegm, reducing its thickness and making it simpler to cough up. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mullein tea contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, helping to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion caused by excess phlegm.

Antimicrobial properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Mullein tea have antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections that can cause excessive mucus production, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.

Expectorant action: The saponins in Mullein tea have a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear out excess phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to break down and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it a natural expectorant.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried mullein leaves and flowers, ideally in early summer morning.
Measure one teaspoon of dried mullein or two teaspoons of fresh mullein for every cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured mullein with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid reaches a rich amber color and fragrance.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, discard solids, and enjoy hot or cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with phlegm because of its natural mucilaginous properties, which soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, making it easier to expel excess mucus. Additionally, plantain's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out sticky phlegm, allowing for a more efficient coughing process. This natural remedy provides relief from congestion, wheezing, and coughs associated with colds, flu, and allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling in the respiratory tract and alleviate congestion, making it easier to expel excess mucus.

Mucilage content: The high mucilage content in Plantain tea helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort while also thickening phlegm, allowing for its easier expulsion.
Expectorant properties: Plantain tea's expectorant compounds help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up excess phlegm and relieve congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots contain mucilages and fiber, making them effective in soothing coughs and relieving congestion.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of plantain have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease respiratory issues associated with phlegm buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves in morning dew or after rain for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop 10-15 grams of cleaned leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors precisely.
Steep the chopped leaves in 500ml of boiling filtered water for 5-7 minutes patiently.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids promptly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy teas helps with phlegm because its natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs and airways. The tea's gentle yet effective soothing qualities can also reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it an excellent natural remedy for coughs and colds. As a result, English ivy teas can provide quick relief from phlegm buildup, helping to promote healthy breathing and clear sinuses.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: Herbal English ivy teas contain saponins, which have been shown to suppress cough reflexes and reduce mucus production, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving coughs associated with phlegm buildup.
Expectorant: The tea's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, allowing for easier breathing and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory: English ivy's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, pneumonia, and other respiratory infections that can lead to excessive phlegm production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to break down and expel mucus from the respiratory tract.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english ivy leaves in the morning or late afternoon for maximum potency.
Rinse 15-20 grams of dried english ivy leaves with cold filtered water to remove impurities.
Steep the english ivy leaves in 250ml boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool and settle for 10-15 minutes before serving warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract. The antispasmodic compounds in thyme, such as thymol, relax the muscles in the lungs and bronchi, making it easier to cough up phlegm and reduce congestion. Additionally, thyme's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to combat infections and reduce swelling in the sinuses and airways, further contributing to its effectiveness in relieving phlegmy coughs and congested chests.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Thyme tea helps to relieve coughs and reduce mucus production by soothing the throat and airways, making it easier to expel excess phlegm.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in thyme tea, such as thymol, help to break down and clear out thick mucus and phlegm from the lungs and bronchial tubes, allowing for easier elimination.

Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tea's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate phlegm buildup in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, thyme roots are effective in relieving congestion and reducing mucus production.
Bark: Thyme bark contains tannins that help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation, making it an excellent addition to teas for phlegm-related issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or use dried thyme flowers.
Rinse the thyme leaves with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the thyme leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine thyme with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with phlegm because its active compound, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, sinuses, and lungs. This reduces congestion and excessive mucus production, allowing the body to naturally clear out phlegm and breathe easier. Additionally, licorice tea's expectorant qualities help loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel through coughing or sneezing, providing relief from chronic coughs and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Licorice root's expectorant property helps loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help ease a sore throat, reduce swelling, and make breathing easier.
Mucilage content: Licorice root contains a type of fiber called mucilage, which forms a protective film over the mucous membranes, soothing and protecting them from irritation and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for phlegm due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe respiratory issues.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to break down mucus and reduce coughing, making it an effective ingredient for teas aimed at relieving phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried licorice root for every cup of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured licorice root with the boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes before straining and serving.
Adjust the sweetness level by adding honey to taste and stirring well.
Serve the tea hot, garnished with lemon slices if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cowslip teas helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways. The tea's active compounds, such as glycosides and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and cough up excess phlegm. Additionally, cowslip tea's mild, sweet flavor makes it a soothing and comforting choice for those looking to ease respiratory issues and find relief from persistent phlegm buildup.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Cowslip tea helps to loosen and clear excess mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Cowslip tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Antiviral properties: Cowslip tea has antiviral properties that help to combat viral infections, such as the common cold and flu, which can cause excessive mucus production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cowslip plant are used in tea form to help reduce excessive mucus production and ease congestion due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the cowslip plant are used in tea form to help break down and eliminate phlegm from the respiratory tract, promoting easier breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cowslip flowers and leaves from your garden or forage area carefully.
Rinse the cowslip flowers and leaves with cold filtered water gently.
Combine the rinsed cowslip flowers and leaves with boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve carefully.
Store the herbal tea in an airtight container and consume within three days refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coltsfoot teas helps with phlegm because its active compounds, such as glycosides and flavonoids, have a natural expectorant property that loosens and clears mucus from the respiratory tract. This can provide relief for individuals suffering from chronic coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues characterized by excessive phlegm production. By promoting the expulsion of phlegm, coltsfoot teas can help alleviate congestion, wheezing, and shortness of breath, ultimately supporting overall lung health and function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Coltsfoot tea helps to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Coltsfoot's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions characterized by excess mucus production.

Mucilaginous properties: The gel-like mucilage found in coltsfoot leaves helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with coughing and clearing phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the coltsfoot plant contain compounds that have been traditionally used to soothe and calm respiratory issues, making them a popular choice for teas aimed at relieving phlegm.
Bark: The bark of the coltsfoot plant has been used for centuries to make teas that help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion caused by excess mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried coltsfoot leaves and flowers according to your personal foraging skills or purchase them from a reputable source.
Rinse the coltsfoot material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris then pat it dry gently with a paper towel.
Combine 2-4 teaspoons of dried coltsfoot leaves and flowers per 8 oz cup of boiling water in a large mug or tea infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Serve the herbal coltsfoot tea hot or cold depending on your personal preference and enjoy its soothing properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow teas helps with phlegm because its natural mucilages help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages, reducing inflammation and congestion. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also work to break down thickened mucus, making it easier to expel, while its expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out excess phlegm from the lungs and airways, promoting a clearer and more comfortable breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: High mallow tea contains a high amount of mucilage, which is a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and coughing caused by excess phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in high mallow tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel excess mucus and alleviate congestion.
Expectorant properties: High mallow tea has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and thin out thick phlegm, making it easier to cough up and clear from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make tea because they contain a high concentration of mucilages, which help to soothe and thin out phlegm.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes are employed in tea preparation due to their ability to reduce inflammation and congestion associated with excess mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried mallow leaves malva sylvestris for the tea preparation.
Measure one teaspoon of dried mallow leaves or two teaspoons of fresh mallow leaves per cup.
Bring filtered water to a boil and pour it over the measured mallow leaves in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes before straining the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Enjoy your soothing high mallow tea hot or iced after sweetening with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry teas helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce congestion and ease respiratory discomfort. The anthocyanins present in elderberries have been shown to inhibit the production of mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving coughs and sore throats. Additionally, elderberry's antiviral properties help combat viral infections that can cause excess mucus production, providing effective relief for those suffering from phlegm-related respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidant compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to phlegm accumulation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce swelling and congestion associated with phlegm buildup, making it easier to expel mucus from the lungs and airways.

Mucolytic properties: Elderberry contains mucolytic compounds that break down and dissolve mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out of the body, which can be especially helpful for individuals with chronic respiratory conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of elderberry contain compounds like inulin and saponins, which have expectorant properties, making them effective at relieving coughs and congestion.
Rhyzomes: Elderberry rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and pneumonia, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra berries and wash them gently with filtered water.
Measure out two tablespoons of dried berries or one tablespoon of fresh berries per cup of boiling water.
Steep the berries in boiling water for 10-15 minutes or until desired color and flavor are achieved.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large ceramic cup.
Allow the tea to cool and sweeten with honey if desired before serving immediately.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat phlegm with herbal teas is by choosing teas with expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Look for herbal ingredients such as licorice root, ginger, thyme, eucalyptus, and slippery elm. These herbs help soothe irritated throats, reduce inflammation, and break up mucus, making it easier to expel. Drink these teas two to three times a day for optimal relief. Remember to consult a healthcare professional before using herbal teas if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have any pre-existing medical conditions.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to phlegm that are treated with herbal teas include respiratory conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and sinus congestion. Herbal teas like thyme, eucalyptus, and ginger are known for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to soothe irritated throat and airways, reduce inflammation, and loosen up mucus. Peppermint tea is also commonly used to alleviate symptoms of congestion and cough, while chamomile tea can help promote relaxation and better sleep. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any herbal remedy, especially for pregnant women, children, and individuals with pre-existing medical conditions.
